summary: Large tournament-mode decomp pass for src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c, replacing many asm stubs with C implementations and upgrading gm_18A5.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures. The PR also fixed a related gm_1601.h prototype, adjusted gmtou declarations, added needed Baselib/menu/player includes, and documented several partially matched functions with explicit match percentages and remaining register-allocation, loop-pattern, or instruction-scheduling problems. Author described the unit as barren and tough, intended as a foundation; there were no review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Main tournament-mode implementation pass; added about 2022 lines and replaced many function stubs with C for menu animation, bracket/player setup, text setup, input handling, scene enter/leave, and match data transitions.
Header cleanup; converted many gm_18A5 prototypes from unknown signatures to typed parameters/returns such as fn_8018F00C(char*, s32), fn_80196684(s32), fn_80196CF8(void), and fn_8019B860(TmData*).
Related prototype correction: fn_801642A0 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool fn_801642A0(void).
Small tournament-mode source adjustment adjacent to gm_18A5 work.
Small tournament-mode header adjustment adjacent to gm_18A5 work.

what_changed: Decompiled numerous gm_18A5 tournament-mode routines that had previously been documented only as stubs, including slot-name formatting, match-end checks, available-slot counting, menu visibility/animation callbacks, input handlers, scene lifecycle callbacks, bracket seeding, match-data initialization, and tournament bracket display setup.

Added concrete implementations for functions visible in the diff excerpt such as fn_8018F00C, gm_8018F1B0, fn_8018F508, fn_8018F888, fn_8018FA24, fn_801902F0, fn_80191154, fn_80191240, fn_80191678, fn_8019175C, fn_801918F0, fn_80191A54, fn_80191B5C, fn_80191CA4, fn_8019249C, fn_80192690, fn_80192758, fn_801949B4, fn_80194BC4, fn_80194D84, fn_80195AF0, fn_8019610C, gm_8019628C_OnFrame, gm_801963B4_OnEnter, gm_801964A4_OnLeave, fn_80196684, fn_80196CF8, fn_80198BA0, fn_80198C60, gm_8019A828, fn_8019B81C, and fn_8019B860.
Added includes required by the new C, including HSD dobj/mobj/particle pieces, gm_1601, mnmainrule, mnname, mnnamenew, and player APIs.
Introduced or refined static state declarations such as lbl_804D663C for SIS text rendering and a more specific static struct for lbl_804799B8 with xC_counter, xE, and xF fields.

Changed tournament slot-name formatting to select US/non-US language templates via lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS, mutate template digits for slot IDs 800-899 and 900-998, and fall back to GetNameText for IDs below 800.
Implemented tournament player setup paths that copy bracket/slot data into TmData-like storage and call Player_SetPlayerCharacter, Player_SetSlottype, and Player_SetCostumeId.
Implemented scene lifecycle behavior: gm_801963B4_OnEnter loads tournament archives/audio resources and gm_801964A4_OnLeave frees archives and resets tournament state fields.
Updated headers to expose more precise function signatures rather than unknown macros, improving downstream type information for other game-mode units.
smart_moves: The PR did not hide imperfect matches: many functions have explicit TODO comments with current match percentages and the suspected blocker, such as register allocation, loop pattern, instruction scheduling, or target store-width issues.
Used targeted casts to force target instruction patterns, with comments noting stw/sth/stb matching needs in functions like fn_8019610C and fn_80198BA0.
Applied #pragma dont_inline around helper functions where inlining would disturb matching, including fn_8018F62C, fn_8018F640, fn_8019044C, and some setup callbacks.
Kept UI-state callbacks factored around gm_8018F634() and gobj->user_data, making the tournament menu state machine easier to follow even where symbol names remain address-based.
Converted a broad set of header prototypes in the same PR as the C implementations, reducing future UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS propagation.

Used comments to label behavioral intent for otherwise address-named callbacks, for example tournament slot display name formatting, SIS text rendering initialization, tournament-mode per-frame update, bracket seeding, and bracket display setup.
decomp_lessons: For barren UI/game-mode units, a foundation PR can be valuable even when several functions remain partial; preserve explicit match percentages and blockers so later workers know where to resume.
Tournament-mode code heavily uses HSD_GObj user_data as small integer state; helpers like fn_8018F62C are useful anchors for callbacks that select animation frames or menu option behavior.
When decompiling menu animation callbacks, representing global byte counters and option-state fields explicitly can clarify repeated HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll patterns.

Header typing should advance with implementation: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete bool, void, s32, HSD_GObj*, MatchEnd*, and TmData* signatures improves compile-time pressure and documents discovered ABI facts.
String/template formatting routines may mutate global language-specific template buffers directly; avoid assuming immutable const strings when the target writes digits into template storage.

For player setup from tournament bracket entries, copied bytes often correspond to character kind, slot type, costume, nametag/team fields, and require calls into Player_* setters to mirror global player state.
Instruction-width matching can require explicit pointer casts to u32* or u16* to produce stw/sth instead of narrower stores; document these casts so they are not simplified away.
naming_conventions: Kept unresolved functions in address-based fn_801xxxxx form while adding behavioral doc comments rather than inventing uncertain semantic names.
Scene callbacks follow the project convention gm_8019628C_OnFrame, gm_801963B4_OnEnter, and gm_801964A4_OnLeave.
Typed tournament structures and parameters use existing names like TmData and MatchEnd where evidence supports them.
Global labels remain lbl_804xxxxx when not fully identified, but local struct fields were refined where usage was clear, such as xC_counter for an animation counter.
Header prototypes preserve address comments such as /* 18F00C */ and pair them with concrete signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline on selected helpers and callbacks to prevent compiler inlining from breaking target codegen.
Used gotos and manually unrolled loop shapes in functions like fn_8018F888 and fn_8018FA24 to approximate target loop/register behavior.
Used PAD_STACK in some functions where stack layout affected matching.
Used explicit casts to match store width, including comments about target stw/sth patterns in fn_80198BA0 and sth patterns in fn_8019610C.
Recorded suspected blockers directly in TODOs: register allocation fixes, loop pattern fixes, minor register allocation fixes, instruction scheduling issues, and significant rework for fn_801981A0.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
Author initially marked the PR as WIP with 'Do not review yet, still WIP'.
Author later noted the unit was 'kinda barren and actually tough' and positioned the work as a foundation, with a caveat that quality checks were attempted but something might have slipped.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve TODO match percentages in gm_18A5.c, especially fn_8018F888, fn_8018FA24, fn_80191240, fn_80196684, and fn_801981A0.
Search for later renames of TmData fields such as cur_option, x20, x2C, x30, x31, x32, and hmn_cpu_count.
Check whether lbl_804799B8, lbl_804799D8, lbl_804D663C, and lbl_804D6670 were later given semantic names or stronger struct types.
Search for follow-up header cleanup removing remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in gm_18A5.h.
Look for later matching tactics around explicit stw/sth/stb casts and #pragma dont_inline in tournament-mode code.
